chunks.title: Introduction
chunks.content: This document provides essential information on asthma management, focusing on inhaled cortic (ICS) and long-acting beta-agonists (LABAs).

chunks.title: Medications - Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
chunks.content: ICSs are the cornerstone of asthma treatment, effective for long-term control by reducing airway inflammation.

chunks.title: Medications - Long-Acting Beta-Agonists (LABAs)
chunks.content: LABAs are used in combination with ICSs for better asthma control, but carry risks if used alone.

chunks.title: Patient Education
chunks.content: Key points for patients include daily use of ICSs, proper technique, and understanding the difference between ICSs and other steroids.

chunks.title: Resources
chunks.content: Provides links to national guidelines and organizations for further information on asthma management.
